artifact_or_test_id: server_impl.py:12716-12783,12942-12980; missing public close fixtures in MemoryAutoPopulateTests
command_or_fixture: Executed public-path forced-failure/re-close probe against current server_impl.py
counterexample_or_failure_condition: A memory failure during the first close reports success, omits memory state, and a second create-mode close does not retry.
expected: The close remains successful but always returns a typed memory outcome and a later idempotent create retries incomplete population.
known_bad_detection_method: Forced the close-time memory helper to fail, then repeated the public create call and counted helper calls.
limitations: Local lifecycle correctness only; no external attacker surface is claimed.
observed: First close returned ok and transitioned_to_closed=true with no memory field; second returned ok and transitioned_to_closed=false; the memory helper was called once.
proposition: Close-time auto-population is observable, fail-isolated, and recoverable without losing the always-on memory supply guarantee.
public_path: wave_close_response(mode='create') twice on the same wave
safety_and_authorization: Temporary fixture repository; no external side effects.

disposition_rationale: Required AC-1/AC-5/AC-7 behavior is silently skipped after a reachable local failure; the repair is bounded to typed result, diagnostic, retry, and public-path fixtures.

command_or_fixture: Compared read_wave_snapshot for live 1sxj7 SQLite state with source_exploration_cost(wave_dir)
counterexample_or_failure_condition: Auto-population reads the older wave.md projection before the close wrapper records and flushes current review/close telemetry.
expected: The stamped cost reflects the current healthy durable telemetry snapshot, with a documented fallback.
known_bad_detection_method: Read both authorities immediately during the review phase and compared the exact debit used for stamping.
limitations: The not-yet-recorded close call may be excluded by design, but already-durable review calls must not be lost.
observed: Live SQLite generation 19 had 19 calls and 15,659 request+response tokens including review; wave.md projection returned 1,381, so close would stamp an 11.3x-low value.
proposition: Close-time memory records stamp the current wave's measured exploration cost.
public_path: memory_supply.source_exploration_cost invoked by wave_close_response
safety_and_authorization: Read-only local telemetry comparison.

disposition_rationale: The wave directly combines auto-population and telemetry; current ordering permanently stamps materially stale provenance. Pass the healthy store snapshot into drafting, with projection fallback, and fixture the newer-store case.

command_or_fixture: Executed placeholder/doc-target probes and a bounded census over all on-disk closed waves
counterexample_or_failure_condition: The gate treats any nonempty target and any summary containing 'Rationale:' as strong, including docs paths and punctuation/placeholder reasons.
expected: Placeholder or non-code decisions remain candidates, and the representative corpus exercises both tiers.
known_bad_detection_method: Constructed valid Decision Log fixtures and evaluated the production pure function; then enumerated the bounded repository corpus.
limitations: This is corpus-quality correctness, not a security threat. Historical waves are used as representative calibration inputs, not auto-written.
observed: Reason '-' became 'Rationale: -.' and promoted; docs/README.md promoted as a code anchor; the census produced 324 drafts across 91 waves and 324/324 auto-promoted, including 167 doc/config targets.
proposition: The deterministic tiering promotes only strong code-anchored decisions with a substantive rationale and leaves thin or ambiguous drafts as candidates.
public_path: memory_supply.draft_candidates plus is_auto_promote
safety_and_authorization: Read-only local fixture and census.

disposition_rationale: The required flood-control/tiering criterion is effectively nonselective on the representative corpus. Define deterministic non-placeholder rationale and actual target-class facts, then run a bounded calibration eval before approval.

command_or_fixture: Exact carrier sweep across seed 004, seed 100, rendered close-wave prompt, memory_supply module header, and wave_close docstring
counterexample_or_failure_condition: Canonical seeds and module/tool descriptions still say supply is human-gated, never auto-promotes, or requires every candidate decided before close, while implementation creates candidates after status becomes closed.
expected: Canonical source and rendered/public carriers describe automatic close writes, promotion, pre-existing-vs-new candidate handling, and the memory response.
known_bad_detection_method: Compared every canonical/rendered carrier named by the change scope to the executed lifecycle order.
limitations: Runtime scripts themselves are packaged automatically; the defect is the installed operating contract, not missing code files.
observed: Seed 004 lines 44-49 says human-gated candidate-only; seed 100 line 98/rendered prompt calls an undecided candidate at close a process gap; memory_supply lines 21-22 says never auto-promotes; wave_close docstring omits new durable writes.
proposition: Fresh installs and upgrades receive operating guidance consistent with close-time automatic supply and promotion.
public_path: framework install/upgrade rendering plus wave_close MCP description
safety_and_authorization: Read-only repository carrier audit.

disposition_rationale: Targets would be upgraded into contradictory instructions. Update canonical seeds/module/tool contract, regenerate surfaces, and add install/upgrade content assertions.

command_or_fixture: Executed live and old-checkpoint normalization probes; inspected test reachability

expected: A permanent fixture drives negative+positive stages through normalization, render, parse, and replace, plus unhealthy zeroing.
known_bad_detection_method: Mapped the two implementation paths to test call sites and executed an old-total checkpoint control.
limitations: No current arithmetic defect reproduced; this finding is a required-path regression gap.
observed: Production paths currently reconcile and direct probes pass, but no committed test reaches the separately edited _normalized_checkpoint_state path; AC-1 cites both builders.
proposition: Both independent telemetry builders and the rendered checkpoint preserve total equals sum of floored stages.
public_path: render_checkpoint_block/parse_checkpoint_block/replace_checkpoint_block
safety_and_authorization: Read-only code/test analysis and temporary checkpoint probe.

disposition_rationale: The duplicated load-bearing calculation needs one bounded public render/parse/replace fixture; otherwise AC evidence overstates coverage.

expected: Run the bounded baseline eval and either implement the widening if proven thin or record an evidence-backed operator narrowing/rejection.

command_or_fixture: Temporary-repository executable probe credited one 500-token memory event, published and compacted the wave, then read the exploration projection again.
counterexample_or_failure_condition: Compaction deletes exploration_credit_event rows while exploration_avoided.read_wave has no floor or closed-wave recovery path.
expected: The post-compaction projection remains 500 with one surfaced event and one credited record.
known_bad_detection_method: Compared the exact public projection before and after the production compaction transaction.
limitations: Local durability/integrity defect; exploration avoided remains an estimate and is not added to measured Context Efficiency.
observed: Before compaction the projection was 500/1/1; compaction returned true; afterward it was 0/0/0.
proposition: Published and compacted waves retain their durable estimated-exploration-avoided projection.
public_path: credit_surface -> mark_checkpoint_published(seal=True) -> compact_published_wave -> exploration_avoided.read_wave
safety_and_authorization: Temporary local repository and SQLite store; no external effects.

disposition_rationale: This wave moved exploration events into the compacted SQLite authority, so losing the published estimate on normal close is a required-contract data-loss defect. Preserve an exploration floor or equivalent closed-wave projection and fixture close/reopen behavior.

command_or_fixture: Instantiated the production PhaseCleanup fixture in a temporary repository, set failed_phase=docs_gate plus dashboard_restart_pending=true/port=7357, supplied a successful dashboard starter, and executed phase_cleanup.
counterexample_or_failure_condition: phase_cleanup skips restart whenever failed_phase is set, then unconditionally removes the upgrade lock that stores dashboard_restart_pending and its port.
expected: Cleanup either restarts the dashboard after an acknowledged recovery or retains the lock/restart intent for the next successful recovery.
known_bad_detection_method: Exercised the exact failed-phase plus restart-pending state omitted by the successful-cleanup and restart-failure tests.
limitations: Local upgrade availability/recovery defect; no remote actor or privilege escalation is claimed.
observed: Dashboard start calls=0, the upgrade lock no longer existed, and the command only reported Upgrade INCOMPLETE.
proposition: If upgrade cutover stopped a running dashboard, failed-upgrade recovery preserves or fulfills the restart intent.
public_path: upgrade-wavefoundry --cleanup over an upgrade lock with failed_phase and dashboard_restart_pending
safety_and_authorization: Temporary repository with mocked dashboard start; no real process was stopped or started.

disposition_rationale: AC-6 explicitly requires restart intent retention on failure. The current cleanup irreversibly loses that state and strands a previously running dashboard; repair and add the missing failed-phase integration fixture.
